Our Story

About Coder

Built in 2026 by three Computer Science students at the University of Wollongong in Dubai — our first company, and a mission to help people understand coding, not just memorise it.

Mission

Why We Built Coder

In 2026, three Computer Science students at the University of Wollongong in Dubai — Ahmed Almarouf, Ted Lokolo, and Midhlaj Abubacker — sat down with a question they had been arguing about for months: "Why do most people who 'learn to code' still not understand how code actually works?"

The answer was painful but clear: courses teach syntax, not understanding. People memorise for loops without ever knowing what the CPU does when it runs one. They copy tutorials without grasping how a program goes from text on a screen to something the computer can execute.

So they started Coder — their first company, and an attempt to fix that gap. The platform teaches everything from scratch: what code is, how it runs, what memory looks like, why functions work, and how the same idea shows up in every language. Then the lessons go all the way up — Python, JavaScript, Java, C++, SQL, React, TypeScript, Git — with a real in-browser compiler so learners can experiment with every concept the moment they read it.

Our goal isn't to graduate people who can copy snippets. It's to graduate people who can read any codebase, debug any bug, and reason about any problem — because they understand what the machine is actually doing.

Leadership

Our Founders

The people who started it all — and still write code every day.
